channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia Faces Revenue Threat Amid U.S. Export Restrictions on AI Chips
item.description: Nvidia faces significant revenue threat due to latest U.S. export restrictions on artificial intelligence chips, analysts and investors said on Monday. The regulations limit AI chip exports to most countries except for select group of close U.S. allies.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft's Plan to Revamp Edge: A New Era for the Web Browser?
item.description: Microsoft's plan to revamp Edge has been flagged up, with potential changes including a dynamic color palette and improved tooltips. Learn more about the Phoenix project and what it means for the browser's future.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bezos 'very optimistic' about Trump's space agenda
item.description: Jeff Bezos, owner of aerospace company Blue Origin, expressed optimism about the incoming Trump administration's space policy, emphasizing Sunday he is not concerned about competitor Elon Musk's allyship with the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Marvel Zombies lands an October release date on Disney Plus, but it won't debut or end on Halloween
item.description: Marvel Zombies, the MCU's first R-rated animated show, will make its Disney Plus bow in early October.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: How AI is helping an Israeli TV reporter speak clearly again
item.description: When a renowned Israeli TV journalist lost his ability to speak clearly because of ALS, he thought his career might be over.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta cutting DEI team, programs in broad shift
item.description: Meta told employees Friday it will cut its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) team and roll back several related programs, marking another major shift in policy for the leading social media company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shut down
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google, Boeing join growing list of companies donating to Trump's inauguration
item.description: Google and Boeing have each given $1 million to President-elect Trump’s inaugural committee, joining a growing list of major corporations supporting the Jan. 20 event. “Google is pleased to support the 2025 inauguration, with a livestream on YouTube and a direct link on our homepage.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Apple doubles down on privacy after Siri-snooping settlement
item.description: Apple has vehemently denied that it ever abused recordings of Siri requests by using those records for marketing, ad sales, or any of the other creepy nonsense we’re being forced to tolerate with other connected devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CompTIA bolsters penetration testing certification
item.description: CompTIA recently upgraded its PenTest+ certification program to educate professionals on cybersecurity penetration testing with training for artificial intelligence (AI), scanning and analysis, and vulnerability management, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use
item.description: TSA has expanded its use of facial recognition technology to more than 80 airports across the country in recent years. The post Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation
